english: Peshwari Naan with coconut and sultanas

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general naan bread entry (FOODON_03302397); no specific Peshwari Naan entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific Peshwari Naan code. A00LC (Bread, leavened) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for Peshwari Naan or stuffed naan variants.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Peshwari Naan piece as served in a restaurant, approximately 120 g, typically consumed as a bread accompaniment to a curry meal.

nutrient_source.title: Naan bread, plain, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18960) adapted with coconut, sultana, and almond filling adjustment using ICMR-NIN compositional data for Indian flatbreads
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Peshwari Naan entry exists in USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or HPB Singapore Foodo. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain naan (NDB 18960, ~317 kcal/100g) adjusted upward for the caloric contribution of desiccated coconut (~354 kcal/100g), sultanas (~299 kcal/100g), and almonds (~579 kcal/100g) at typical filling proportions (approximately 15% coconut, 10% sultanas, 8% almonds by weight of filling). The resulting composite estimate yields approximately 340 kcal/100g. ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) was cross-referenced for Indian flatbread macros. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; plain naan bread GI reference used as analogous estimate for Peshwari Naan
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Peshwari Naan found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. Plain naan bread has a reported GI of approximately 71 (high) per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables and the University of Sydney GI Database. Peshwari Naan contains added coconut, sultanas, and almonds; the fat and fibre from these ingredients may modestly lower the effective GI, but the high refined-flour base and sugar content from sultanas are expected to maintain a high GI category. GI of 71 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (71 × 63.6) / 100 ≈ 45.2 for a 120 g serving (53.0 g carb/100g × 1.2 = 63.6 g carb per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg/100g) and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Peshwari Naan to half a piece per meal, as a 120 g serving contributes approximately 456 mg sodium, representing about 20% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; pairing with low-sodium curries is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~71) and high glycemic load per serving (~45), driven by refined wheat flour and added sultanas
type2_diabetes.guidance: Peshwari Naan is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high GI of approximately 71 and a glycemic load of approximately 45 per standard serving, which will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike; if consumed, portion should be restricted to no more than half a piece and paired with high-fibre, low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~4.8 g/100g) from coconut and ghee, and refined carbohydrate density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Peshwari Naan occasionally and in small portions, as the saturated fat from desiccated coconut and ghee (~5.8 g per 120 g serving) may contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation when consumed regularly; choosing plain naan or whole-wheat alternatives is preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peshwari Naan is a leavened Indian flatbread originating from the Peshawar region of present-day Pakistan and the North-West Frontier of the Indian subcontinent. It is made from white wheat flour dough that is leavened with yeast or baking powder, enriched with yogurt and ghee, and stuffed with a sweet filling of desiccated coconut, sultanas (or raisins), and ground or flaked almonds before being baked in a tandoor clay oven at high heat. The result is a soft, slightly chewy bread with a golden-brown exterior and a sweet, nutty, fragrant interior. The coconut provides a mild tropical sweetness, the sultanas add a fruity chewiness, and the almonds contribute a subtle crunch and richness. Peshwari Naan is typically served as an accompaniment to Indian curries, particularly mild or creamy dishes such as butter chicken or korma, where its sweetness complements the savoury sauce. In Singapore, it is available at Indian restaurants and tandoori specialists. Nutritionally, a standard 120 g piece provides approximately 408 kcal, 9.8 g protein, 11.4 g fat (including ~5.8 g saturated fat from coconut and ghee), and 63.6 g carbohydrate with 15 g sugar. The high glycemic index (~71) and high glycemic load (~45 per serving) make it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~456 mg per serving) warrants moderation for those with hypertension. The saturated fat from coconut and ghee is a consideration for cardiovascular health. Allergens include gluten, tree nuts (almonds), dairy, and sulphites from sultanas.

confidence_reason: No exact Peshwari Naan entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain naan with compositional adjustment for the coconut, sultana, and almond filling based on typical recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from plain naan data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Medium confidence is appropriate given the adaptation methodology.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates; no exact Peshwari Naan entry exists in any approved primary database. Values should be updated if a direct measurement becomes available in ICMR-NIN or HPB Foodo. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy naan entry or ICMR-NIN flatbread data. 3. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this food. 4. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate based on plain naan; actual GI of Peshwari Naan may be slightly lower due to fat and fibre from coconut and almonds moderating glucose absorption, but remains in the high category. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical restaurant preparation; individual restaurant sourcing should be verified. 6. Kosher status is not confirmed. 7. The food is served at 2 Singapore field survey restaurants: Indian House/Dumpukht Grills & Curry (Boat Quay) and Zaffron Tandoori (Boat Quay). Price range SGD 4.13–5.90 per piece. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is an Indian dish served in Singapore. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy naan and ICMR-NIN flatbread compositional data as part of the adapted estimate.
